title: Police: Mass. man allegedly stabbed friend during fight over woman at Miami Beach bachelor party
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Olajuwon Shawn Dickerson, 32, from Marston Mills, was charged with aggravated battery after allegedly stabbing his friend in Miami Beach.
The incident occurred during a bachelor party, where a fight over women led to a stabbing involving a folding knife.
Brian Gordon, stabbed in the abdomen, was hospitalized and released; Dickerson was arrested and held without bond.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-1 loss to St. Louis City SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution lost 3-1 to St. Louis City SC, falling to their third defeat in four games.
Ilay Feingold scored the opening goal, but St. Louis responded with three unanswered goals.
Revolution forward Leo Campana exited the match with a new injury; severity pending an MRI.

title: Don’t be fooled by this fake court notice scam in Mass., NH, authorities warn
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Authorities in Massachusetts and New Hampshire warn of fake court notices sent by scammers demanding payment for traffic violations.
Notices include errors such as incorrect state names, generic judge names, and incorrect court phone numbers.
Residents are advised not to scan QR codes or share personal details and to verify with official court contacts.

title: The trial of Kelsey Fitzsimmons begins this week. Here’s what to know.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Trial for Kelsey Fitzsimmons, accused of assault with a weapon, begins March 23 without a jury in Essex Superior Court.
Fitzsimmons, a North Andover officer, was shot by a colleague while being served a restraining order at her home.
Conflicting accounts surround the shooting, with Fitzsimmons alleging false claims from her fiancé, who sought custody of their child.

title: She’s running Boston for patients ‘rebuilding their lives step by step’
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Lina Sinisterra Mazariegos, 44, from Cali, Colombia, is running the Boston Marathon for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
Sinisterra Mazariegos views running as a reflection of patience and perseverance, similar to recovering patients at Spaulding.
Spaulding Rehabilitation helps individuals regain independence and confidence; Sinisterra Mazariegos runs to support their journey.

title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives and The Chats performed at the House of Blues, showcasing high energy and audience engagement.
Frontman Howlin’ Pelle Almqvist led with charisma, integrating new and old songs with enthusiasm and lively performances.
The Hives' setlist featured songs like 'Hate To Say I Told You So' and 'The Hives Forever Forever The Hives.'

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, from Braintree is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
Diagnosed with Stage 3 breast cancer in 2023, McHugh resumed running after chemo, surgeries, and therapy.
McHugh honors her late friend Susan Hurley and uncle Kenny McHugh by participating in this marathon.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Senator Ed Markey urges Meta to address privacy concerns over facial recognition in smart glasses.
Markey, with Senators Wyden and Merkley, warns glasses could track and identify protesters without consent.
Meta has until April 6 to respond to questions about data collection and user privacy safeguards.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy City Council delayed a $2.65 million proposal to replace firefighting gear containing harmful PFAS chemicals.
Mayor Thomas Koch requested funding for PFAS-free gear due to health risks associated with the current equipment.
Firefighters expressed disappointment over the delay, citing cancer risks and safety concerns from prolonged PFAS exposure.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Attleboro proposes transforming Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve and wildlife rehabilitation center to reduce costs.
The zoo requires $57 million in repairs and costs the city $900,000 annually, facing declining attendance.
Zoo leaders express concern over the transition plan, inviting public feedback as the proposal progresses.
